verses.text: David sets out to bring the ark to Jerusalem with thirty thousand men, music, and great joy. But they carry it on a new cart — the Philistines' way, not the way God commanded, on the shoulders of the Levites. Zeal is not enough; God must be served in God's own way.

David sets out to bring the ark to Jerusalem with thirty thousand men, music, and great joy. But they carry it on a new cart — the Philistines' way, not the way God commanded, on the shoulders of the Levites. Zeal is not enough; God must be served in God's own way.

David sets out to bring the ark to Jerusalem with thirty thousand men, music, and great joy. But they carry it on a new cart — the Philistines' way, not the way God commanded, on the shoulders of the Levites. Zeal is not enough; God must be served in God's own way.

David sets out to bring the ark to Jerusalem with thirty thousand men, music, and great joy. But they carry it on a new cart — the Philistines' way, not the way God commanded, on the shoulders of the Levites. Zeal is not enough; God must be served in God's own way.

When the oxen stumble and Uzzah reaches out to steady the ark, God strikes him dead on the spot. The blow seems harsh, yet the ark was never to be touched or hauled like cargo. Familiarity with holy things can breed a carelessness that forgets how holy God is.

When the oxen stumble and Uzzah reaches out to steady the ark, God strikes him dead on the spot. The blow seems harsh, yet the ark was never to be touched or hauled like cargo. Familiarity with holy things can breed a carelessness that forgets how holy God is.

David is angry, then afraid: 'How shall the ark of the LORD come to me?' He leaves it at Obed-edom's house — and there God blesses that household richly. The same presence that struck Uzzah blesses the reverent. God is a terror to the careless, a joy to the humble.

David is angry, then afraid: 'How shall the ark of the LORD come to me?' He leaves it at Obed-edom's house — and there God blesses that household richly. The same presence that struck Uzzah blesses the reverent. God is a terror to the careless, a joy to the humble.

Learning of Obed-edom's blessing, David brings up the ark rightly this time, with sacrifices along the way, and dances before the LORD with all his might. The king lays aside his dignity to worship. Real joy in God is not too proud to look undignified before Him.

Learning of Obed-edom's blessing, David brings up the ark rightly this time, with sacrifices along the way, and dances before the LORD with all his might. The king lays aside his dignity to worship. Real joy in God is not too proud to look undignified before Him.

Michal, Saul's daughter, watches from a window and 'despised him in her heart' for dancing like a commoner. Where all Israel rejoiced, she sneered. A cold heart scorns the very abandon of worship that heaven itself delights to see.

The ark set in its tent, David offers sacrifices, blesses the people in the LORD's name, and gives every man and woman bread and meat and wine. Worship overflows into generosity. A heart full of God becomes a hand open to others.

The ark set in its tent, David offers sacrifices, blesses the people in the LORD's name, and gives every man and woman bread and meat and wine. Worship overflows into generosity. A heart full of God becomes a hand open to others.

The ark set in its tent, David offers sacrifices, blesses the people in the LORD's name, and gives every man and woman bread and meat and wine. Worship overflows into generosity. A heart full of God becomes a hand open to others.

Michal mocks David's display, but he answers that he will be yet more 'vile' in his own eyes to honour the LORD who chose him. She remains childless to her death. To despise wholehearted worship is to choose barrenness of soul; David would rather be lowly before God than grand before men.
